remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Sustained weight loss of >5% without intervention: ~5-10% at 2 years; weight regain almost universal after behavioural intervention alone (95% return to within 5% of starting weight at 5 years on lifestyle alone)
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide 2.4mg): 15-17% mean weight loss at 68 weeks (STEP 1); tirzepatide (dual GIP/GLP-1): 20-22% at 72 weeks (SURMOUNT-1); bariatric surgery: 25-35% sustained weight loss at 10 years with metabolic syndrome resolution
remission.gap_size: Very large — effective pharmacotherapy now exists but is massively under-prescribed; access is the primary barrier
remission.barrier_detail: Obesity is still widely treated as a lifestyle choice rather than a chronic disease by payers; GLP-1 medications (semaglutide, tirzepatide) cost $1,000-1,300/month and are excluded from most Medicare coverage; stigma in clinical settings reduces treatment-seeking; bariatric surgery used in <1% of eligible patients
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — tirzepatide (Zepbound) FDA approved for obesity; 2021 semaglutide (Wegovy) approved; 2024 semaglutide approved for CV risk reduction in obesity without diabetes (SELECT trial: 20% CV event reduction); prior obesity medications (orlistat, phentermine) essentially unchanged since 1990s-2000s
remission.outdated_treatments: Phentermine monotherapy (approved 1959, short-term only; often used long-term off-label without evidence); appetite suppressants (fenfluramine/phen-fen — withdrawn 1997 for valvulopathy; some of same class derivatives re-emerging with different risk profiles); 'diet and exercise counselling' alone without pharmacotherapy or surgical referral in severe obesity
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Time-restricted eating (TRE) — independent of caloric restriction; resistance training (preserves muscle during GLP-1 induced weight loss); cagrilintide (amylin analogue) combination with semaglutide (CagriSema — Phase 3: 22.7% weight loss); endoscopic sleeve gastroplasty
remission.frontier_signal: Amycretin (GLP-1/amylin co-agonist — Phase 2: 22% weight loss at 36 weeks); retatrutide (triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon — Phase 2: 24.2% at 48 weeks, most effective agent to date); oral semaglutide for obesity (Phase 3 underway); bimagrumab (anti-ActRII — recomposes body without total weight loss)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
